Windows "Could Not Find This Item" — When a File or Folder Refuses to Delete

I was working on Windows recently and hit a strange situation — a folder I couldn't delete. It's a classic Windows pain point that's been around since at least Windows 7 and is still alive in Windows 10. Writing it up.
You right-click a folder or file, pick Delete, and get this:
Doesn't matter what you try — right-click delete, the Delete key, Shift + Delete — same error. And now you've got a folder or file sitting there refusing to leave. Here's how to clear it.
Something else (another app) is holding the file/folder open
The disk has a bad sector
This one's easier. Find the app holding the file/folder open and close it.
Easiest way is just to fire up Task Manager and kill suspicious processes. If you don't know which one, the usual culprits are:
Cloud sync agents (Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, etc.)
Closing one of those usually does it. If you really can't figure out which, just restart the machine.
"Modern Family" Will (played by Dwayne Johnson): "When in doubt, reboot."
This one's trickier — lots of possible root causes (unplugging the drive without ejecting, power loss during a file transfer, etc.). Painful to debug.
Easiest first try is Windows' built-in disk check — let it scan and auto-repair. If that doesn't fix it, drop to a command prompt:
If even that fails, you're into third-party tools — Unlocker is the classic. I won't go deep on it, look it up if you need it.
Safe Mode is another worth trying — with fewer processes running, the file lock often isn't there to block the delete.

Recently hit a familiar Windows annoyance — try to delete a file or folder, and Windows says "Could not find this item". I've been seeing this since Windows 7, and it's still around on Windows 10. This post covers the two common causes (a process holding the file open vs. disk sector trouble) and the easy ways to fix each.
